data.title: Crypto Funds Bleed $1.80 Billion As Metals Rally Heats Up
data.description:
Investors stepped back this week as a mix of shifting bets and quick profit-taking pushed money out of spot crypto ETFs. Markets moved fast, and some of the biggest swings were driven by short-term reactions rather than a change in long-term views. Related Reading: Ethereum Boost: Vitalik Buterin Sets Aside $45M In ETH For Privacy And Open Tech Spot Crypto ETF Flows Based on reports from Farside, US-based spot Bitcoin ETFs saw about $1. 50 billion leave over five trading days, while spot Ether ETFs had roughly $327 million in outflows
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/crypto-funds-bleed-1-80-billion-as-metals-rally-heats-up/

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Pressure as Long-Term Holders Sell at Fastest Rate Since August
data.description: Bitcoin has recently breached a significant support level, with long-term holders selling at their quickest pace since August. According to NS3.AI, this change in behavior among holders has sparked a debate among industry experts. Some analysts caution that this trend could lead to further price declines, while others suggest it might signal a potential bottom in the bear market. The increased selling activity by long-term holders may exert additional downward pressure on Bitcoin's price in the short term.

data.title: In the past hour, $650 million in trades were liquidated across the entire network, with the largest single liquidation amounting to approximately $13.39 million.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, approximately $650 million in contract liquidations occurred across the entire network in the past hour, with about $630 million in long positions and about $22.99 million in short positions. The largest single liquidation occurred in an ETH position on Hyperliquid, amounting to approximately $13.3899 million.

data.title: NAV Struck at London Close Raises Questions
data.description: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF Analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, posted on X about the Net Asset Value (NAV) being struck at the London close, which corresponds to noon U.S. time. This timing has sparked discussions and raised questions among market observers. The unusual timing of the NAV calculation has been described as 'crazy' by some, indicating a level of surprise or concern within the financial community. The implications of this timing on market operations and investor decisions remain a topic of interest.

data.title: Wintermute’s founder defends Binance amid October 10 flash crash blame game
data.description:

The controversy involving cryptocurrency exchanges over last October’s market crash has […]
